marketFocus.focusTagline: Gold ATHs / Fed Non-Event / Iran Tensions
headlines: Spot Gold extends to record $5312/oz (+2.7%) in Asia-Pac, Silver also bid
Fed held 3.50-3.75% as expected, Powell sees goods inflation peak mid-year
Bessent affirms 'strong USD policy', downplays JPY intervention talk
Trump: 'Massive Armada heading to Iran'—WTI $63, Brent $68
marketFocus.macroContext: FOMC delivered a non-event hold at 3.50-3.75% with 2 dissenters (Waller/Miran for 25bp cut). Powell struck an improved tone, noting goods inflation should peak mid-year and no rate hike in base case. Bessent's 'strong USD' rhetoric clashed with Tuesday's de-dollarisation selloff, but USD clawed back ground. Gold surged to fresh ATHs on weaker USD and geopolitical risk (Iran). Crude rallied on Trump's Iran comments before paring gains as Iran signaled readiness for talks.

tradingSummary: USD stabilized post-FOMC but remains fragile after Tuesday's 97.28→95.55 plunge. AUD outperformed on firmer CPI (3.8% vs 3.6% exp), with major banks backing Feb RBA hike (70%+ priced). EUR held below 1.20, awaiting next week's ECB. GBP pulled back from 1.3869 highs. JPY consolidated after breaking 100DMA (153.65). Gold's historic run continues—safe-haven + USD weakness narrative intact. Crude choppy on Iran headlines.

USD.headline: Bessent talks strong USD, but flows skeptical
USD.summary: Fed non-event (3.50-3.75% hold, 2 dissent for cut). Powell: goods inflation peaks mid-year, no hike base case—dovish tilt. Bessent 'strong USD policy' rhetoric clashed with Tuesday's de-dollarisation plunge (97.28→95.55). DXY clawed back to 96.00-96.35 range, but fragile. Real yields flat, 2Y ~4.25%. Gold ATHs signal USD credibility erosion. Tactical bounce exhausted—bias weak 1-3 days unless data surprises.

EUR.headline: ECB meeting next week caps upside
EUR.summary: EUR/USD eased below 1.20 (1.1952-1.2045 range) after touching 1.2082 Tuesday—strongest since mid-2021. USD weakness main driver, not EUR strength. ECB gathering next week: market watches for downside inflation risk hints. Bund yields grinding higher (+25 ticks), supply robust but demand tepid. No CB rhetoric today (Simkus/Villeroy ignored). Holding gains, but topside capped pre-ECB. Range 1.19-1.21 for 1-3 days.

GBP.headline: BoE meeting next week—pullback healthy
GBP.summary: GBP/USD pulled back from Tuesday's 1.3869 peak (last seen 2021), little fresh domestic news. BoE meeting next week in focus—no cuts priced, inflation sticky. Gilt yields firm (4.60%+ 10Y), rate differential supports GBP. Softer USD backdrop keeps Sterling bid. Pullback to 1.37-1.38 zone is consolidation, not reversal. Bias still bullish 1-3 days—buy dips ahead of BoE.

JPY.headline: Bessent downplays intervention—carry flows win
JPY.summary: USD/JPY consolidated 152.14-153.07 after breaking 100DMA (153.65) Tuesday. Bessent: 'no JPY intervention' talk—removed support for JPY bulls. JGB 10Y ~1.25%, real yields negative. BoJ rhetoric ignored (minutes showed 'appropriate to raise rates'). Carry unwind risk exists, but flows still short JPY. Trump FX noise adds volatility. Bias weak 1-3 days—sell rallies toward 154.

AUD.headline: Hot CPI backs Feb RBA hike—major banks on board
AUD.summary: AUD outperformed on firmer CPI: 3.8% YoY (vs 3.6% exp), Trimmed Mean 3.3% (in line but above RBA 2-3% target). Major banks (ANZ, Westpac, CBA, NAB) backed Feb RBA hike—markets price 70%+ probability. Iron ore stable ~$100/t, China PMI soft but stimulus hopes intact. AUD/USD briefly higher, holding gains. RBA meeting Feb 18—hike narrative building. Bias bullish 1-3 days, buy dips.

CAD.headline: BoC hold + Macklem tariff focus—range-bound
CAD.summary: BoC held rates (as expected), MPR saw CPI Q2/Q3 unchanged, Q4 raised. Macklem statement focused on tariff risks—no urgency to cut. USD/CAD unreactive, trading 1.3535-1.3615 range. Oil $63 WTI—no tailwind from energy. Trump tariff threats (25% autos/pharma) priced in Tuesday. No fresh catalyst—CAD stuck in range. Bias neutral 1-3 days, slight bearish tilt if USD recovers.

CHF.headline: No independent drivers—tracks EUR
CHF.summary: CHF mechanically follows EUR moves. SNB last cut Dec, next meeting March. Inflation 0.6%—well below target. No flight-to-safety flows despite Iran tensions (Gold took safe-haven bid instead). EUR/CHF range-bound, no CB rhetoric. Dead money 1-3 days—avoid unless hedging EUR exposure.

NZD.headline: RBNZ dovish, dairy soft—AUD proxy
NZD.summary: RBNZ cut 50bp Jan 22 (4.25%)—more cuts priced (3.50% by mid-year). Dairy auction prices -1.2%—headwind. No domestic data until Feb. China stimulus hopes provide floor. NZD follows AUD/risk sentiment—no independent edge. ANZ Business Confidence tonight (01:00) unlikely to move needle. Range-bound 1-3 days.
